== 01 — Janet in Neovim: Setup

This guide walks you through installing Janet and activating the Neovim
toolchain that is already configured in this repository — LSP, REPL,
structural editing, formatting, and rainbow delimiters.

____
*Series:* 01 Setup ← you are here · xref:learning/janet/02-first-steps.adoc[02 First
Steps]
____

'''''

== 1. Install Janet

Janet has no runtime dependencies and ships as a single binary.

[width="100%",cols="50%,50%",options="header",]
|===
|Platform |Command
|*Ubuntu / Debian* |Build from source — see
https://janet-lang.org/[janet-lang.org]

|*Arch Linux* |`sudo pacman -S janet`

|*macOS* |`brew install janet`

|*Windows* |Download from https://janet-lang.org/[janet-lang.org]
|===

Verify after installing:

[source,sh]
----
janet -v
----

'''''

== 2. Install jpm (Janet Package Manager)

`jpm` is the standard package manager for Janet libraries and tools. It
is bundled with most Janet installations. Confirm it is available:

[source,sh]
----
which jpm
----

If it is missing, bootstrap it manually:

[source,sh]
----
git clone https://github.com/janet-lang/jpm.git
cd jpm
janet bootstrap.janet
----

See https://github.com/janet-lang/jpm[janet-lang/jpm] for full bootstrap
instructions.

'''''

== 3. Install the LSP Server

`janet-lsp` provides completions, hover documentation, go-to-definition,
and inline diagnostics inside Neovim.

[source,sh]
----
jpm install janet-lsp
----

`jpm` installs binaries to `~/.jpm/bin/`. Add that to your `$PATH` if it
is not already there:

[source,sh]
----
# Add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc
export PATH="$HOME/.jpm/bin:$PATH"
----

Verify Neovim can find the binary:

[source,sh]
----
which janet-lsp
----

'''''

== 4. How the Neovim Plugins Are Wired

Everything is pre-configured. When you open any `.janet` file the
following plugins load automatically:

[width="100%",cols="34%,33%,33%",options="header",]
|===
|Plugin |What it does |Config file
|*Conjure* |Spawns a Janet REPL; evaluates code without leaving the
editor |`lua/plugins/lisp.lua`

|*vim-sexp* {plus} *mappings* |Structural editing — slurp, barf, move
S-expressions |`lua/plugins/lisp.lua`

|*nvim-parinfer* |Keeps parentheses balanced as you edit indentation
|`lua/plugins/lisp.lua`

|*rainbow-delimiters.nvim* |Colour-codes matching brackets by nesting
depth |`lua/plugins/lisp.lua`

|*conform.nvim* |Format-on-save (via LSP) {plus} `++<++leader++>++f`
manual format |`lua/plugins/conform.lua`

|*janet++_++lsp* |LSP completions, hover, go-to-definition, diagnostics
|`lua/config/lsp.lua`
|===

Filetype-specific settings (lisp indent, spell off, `localleader = ,`)
live in `after/ftplugin/janet.lua`.

'''''

== 5. Verify the Setup

[arabic]
. Open (or create) a Janet file:
+
[source,sh]
----
nvim hello.janet
----
. Write a minimal expression:
+
[source,janet]
----
(print "Hello from Janet!")
----
. Evaluate the buffer with `,eb` — the Conjure log should print
`Hello from Janet!`.
. Check LSP is attached: `:LspInfo` → you should see `janet++_++lsp`
listed as active for the current buffer.
. Hover over a symbol and press `K` — a documentation popup should
appear.

'''''

== 6. Troubleshooting

[width="100%",cols="34%,33%,33%",options="header",]
|===
|Symptom |Likely cause |Fix
|Conjure never starts a REPL |`janet` not on `$PATH` |`which janet`;
install Janet or fix your PATH

|No completions / hover |`janet-lsp` not found |`which janet-lsp`; add
`~/.jpm/bin` to PATH

|LSP shows as inactive |Server crashed |`:LspLog` for details; check
`janet-lsp` binary works standalone

|Parinfer fights your edits |Working against the paradigm |Edit
_indentation_, not parens — parinfer infers parens from indent
|===

For deeper LSP debugging see `docs/cheatsheets/lsp.md` and run
`:LspInfo` / `:LspLog` inside Neovim.

'''''
